div1.entryFree:
Unsure (unsúre; but before a subst. either únsure or unsúre, according as the subst. is accentuated: únsure hópes; unsúre obsérvance) 1) unsafe, liable to danger: an habitation giddy and u. H4B I, 3, 89. exposing what is mortal and u. to all that fortune, death and danger dare, Hml. IV, 4, 51.
2) not assured, not certainly knowing, liable to error: the truth thou art u. to swear, John III, 1, 283. his scattering and u. observance, Oth. III, 3, 151.
3) uncertain, doubtful: what's to come is still u. Tw. II, 3, 50. their u. hopes, Mcb. V, 4, 19.
